content.problemStatement:
Alice has a very complex machine ‚Äē when fed with a sequence A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč, it produces a sequence B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1‚Äč,B2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,BN‚Äč, where for each valid iii, BiB_iBi‚Äč is the largest index jjj such that AiA_iAi‚Äč divides AjA_jAj‚Äč (since AiA_iAi‚Äč divides itself, such an index always exist). For example, if the machine is fed with A=[2,6,5,3,4]A = [2, 6, 5, 3, 4]A=[2,6,5,3,4], it produces B=[5,2,3,4,5]B = [5, 2, 3, 4, 5]B=[5,2,3,4,5].

Alice gave you a sequence BBB that was produced by the machine. Find an integer sequence AAA such that when it is fed into the machine, then the machine produces the sequence BBB. Alice does not like huge integers, so make sure that 1‚Č§Ai‚Č§4‚čÖ1061 \le A_i \le 4 \cdot 10^61‚Č§Ai‚Äč‚Č§4‚čÖ106 holds for each valid iii.
constraints: 1‚Č§Bi‚Č§N1 \le B_i \le N1‚Č§Bi‚Äč‚Č§N for each valid iii
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚čÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚čÖ105
Subtasks**Subtask #1 (20 points):** B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1‚Äč,B2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,BN‚Äč are pairwise distinct
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1‚Äč,B2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,BN‚Äč.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case, print a single line containing NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč. For each valid iii, 1‚Č§Ai‚Č§4‚čÖ1061 \le A_i \le 4 \cdot 10^61‚Č§Ai‚Äč‚Č§4‚čÖ106 should hold.

If there are multiple solutions, you may print any of them. It is guaranteed that at least one solution exists.
